heading: Consumer product safety standard to protect against tip-over of clothing storage units
label: 2056f Consumer product safety standard to protect against tip-over of clothing storage units
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023, and not as part of the Consumer Product Safety Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Clothing storage unit defined
children.label: a Clothing storage unit defined
children.text: In this section, the term “clothing storage unit” means any free-standing furniture item manufactured in the United States or imported for use in the United States that is intended for the storage of clothing, typical of bedroom furniture.

children.heading: CPSC determination of scope
children.label: b CPSC determination of scope
children.text: The Consumer Product Safety Commission shall specify the types of furniture items within the scope of subsection (a) as part of a standard promulgated under this section based on tip-over data as reasonably necessary to protect children up to 72 months of age from injury or death.

children.heading: Consumer product safety standard required
children.label: c Consumer product safety standard required
A
children.text: in consultation with representatives of consumer groups, clothing storage unit manufacturers, craft or handmade furniture manufacturers, and independent child product engineers and experts, examine and assess the effectiveness of any voluntary consumer product safety standards for clothing storage units; and

children.label: B
children.text: in accordance with and paragraph (2), promulgate a final consumer product safety standard for clothing storage units to protect children from tip-over-related death or injury, that shall take effect 180 days after the date of promulgation or such a later date as the Commission determines appropriate.section 553 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: tests that simulate the weight of children up to 60 pounds;

children.label: B
children.text: objective, repeatable, reproducible, and measurable tests or series of tests that simulate real-world use and account for impacts on clothing storage unit stability that may result from placement on carpeted surfaces, drawers with items in them, multiple open drawers, and dynamic force;

children.label: C
children.text: testing of all clothing storage units, including those 27 inches and above in height; and

children.label: D
children.text: warning requirements based on ASTM F2057–19, or its successor at the time of enactment, provided that the Consumer Product Safety Commission may strengthen the warning requirements of ASTM F2057–19, or its successor, if reasonably necessary to protect children from tip-over-related death or injury.

children.heading: Testing clarification
children.label: 3 Testing clarification
children.text: Tests referred to in paragraph (2)(B) shall allow for the utilization of safety features (excluding tip restraints) to work as intended if the features cannot be overridden by consumers in normal use.

children.heading: Treatment of standard
children.label: 4 Treatment of standard
children.text: A consumer product safety standard promulgated under paragraph (1) shall be treated as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under .section 2058 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a voluntary standard exists that meets the requirements of paragraph (2), the Commission shall, not later than 90 days after the date on which such determination is made and in accordance with , promulgate a final consumer product safety standard that adopts the applicable performance requirements of such voluntary standard related to protecting children from tip-over-related death or injury. A consumer product safety standard promulgated under this subsection shall be treated as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under . Such standard shall take effect 120 days after the date of the promulgation of the rule, or such a later date as the Commission determines appropriate. Such standard will supersede any other existing standard for clothing storage units to protect children from tip-over-related death or injury.section 553 of title 5section 2058 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: protects children up to 72 months of age from tip-over-related death or injury;

children.label: B
children.text: meets the requirements described in subsection (c)(2);

children.label: C
children.text: is, or will be, published not later than 60 days after ; andDecember 29, 2022

children.label: D
children.text: is developed by ASTM International or such other standard development organization that the Commission determines is in compliance with the intent of this section.

children.heading: Notice required to be published in the Federal Register
children.label: 3 Notice required to be published in the Federal Register
children.text: The Commission shall publish a notice in the Federal Register upon beginning the promulgation of a rule under this subsection.

children.heading: Notice to commission
children.label: 1 Notice to commission
children.text: If the performance requirements of a voluntary standard adopted under subsection (d) are subsequently revised, the organization that revised the performance requirements of such standard shall notify the Commission of such revision after final approval.

children.heading: Treatment of revision
children.label: 2 Treatment of revision
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the Commission is notified of revised performance requirements of a voluntary standard described in paragraph (1) (or such later date as the Commission determines appropriate), the Commission shall determine whether the revised performance requirements meet the requirements of subsection (d)(2)(B), and if so, modify, in accordance with , the standard promulgated under subsection (d) to include the revised performance requirements that the Commission determines meet such requirements. The modified standard shall take effect after 180 days or such later date as the Commission deems appropriate.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Beginning 5 years after , subsequent to the publication of a consumer product safety standard under this section, the Commission may, at any time, initiate rulemaking, in accordance with , to modify the requirements of such standard or to include additional provisions if the Commission makes a determination that such modifications or additions are reasonably necessary to protect children from tip-over-related death or injury.December 29, 2022section 553 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: whether the petition meets the requirements for petitions set forth in section 1051.5 of title 16, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation implementing ; andsection 2058(i) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the petition demonstrates that the test could reasonably meet the requirements of subsection (c)(2)(B), and if so, the Commission shall determine by recorded vote, within 60 days after the determination, whether to initiate rulemaking, in accordance with , to revise a consumer product safety standard promulgated under this section to include the new or revised test.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Demonstration of compliance
children.label: B Demonstration of compliance
children.text: Compliance with the testing requirements of a standard revised under subparagraph (A) may be demonstrated either through the performance of a new or revised test under subparagraph (A) or the performance of the tests otherwise required under a standard promulgated under this section.

children.heading: Treatment of rules
children.label: 3 Treatment of rules
children.text: Any rule promulgated under this subsection, including any modification or revision made under this subsection, shall be treated as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under .section 2058 of this title
